title: pogogyne douglasii var. parviflora
intro: Pogogyne douglasii var. parviflora, also known as Douglas' Pogogyne or Little-flowered Pogogyne, is a plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the Pacific coast of North America. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Pogogyne douglasii var. parviflora are attributed to its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
